title: Dacrycarpus kinabaluensis
taxonomy: Dacrycarpus kinabaluensis is a species of coniferous tree in the family Podocarpaceae. This plant is native to Borneo, specifically in the Kinabalu National Park in Sabah, Malaysia. The classification of Dacrycarpus kinabaluensis includes being a member of the genus Dacrycarpus and the family Podocarpaceae. The family Podocarpaceae is a group of coniferous trees commonly known as yellowwoods or podocarps. Dacrycarpus kinabaluensis is a unique and endemic species within its range.

intro: Dacrycarpus kinabaluensis, also known as the Kinabalu papiricarpus or Sabah's weeping podocarp, is a species of coniferous tree native to Borneo. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making it useful in the management of various ailments such as arthritis and oxidative stress. Bioactive constituents of Dacrycarpus kinabaluensis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
